label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we can infer from Toy Fair 2014. The options are A, B, C, D. I need to read the passage again and find the clues. First, the article mentions that 2014's trends include oversized toys. It says, "In 2014, everything is really big and really out there," according to an expert. Also, they mention a trend called "Larger Than Life" which is about big toys with a big play value. So that seems to point towards option C, big toys being popular. Then, looking at the other options. Option A says toys are becoming more educational than fun. The passage does mention a push towards education with STEAM, but it also says "though it is all fun and games..." So maybe they are both fun and educational? The article doesn't say that educational aspects are more than fun, just that there's a push towards education. So maybe A is not the correct inference. Option B: Robots are great favorites of buyers. The article says they saw some super cool, high-tech robots and that technology includes robots. But does it say they are the great favorites? It's a favorite trend, but maybe not the biggest. The "Larger Than Life" trend was a favorite, and robots are part of the tech trend. But the question is about what we can infer. Maybe B is possible, but maybe not the best choice. Option D: Simple card games will disappear on the market.
